title: SOA-Harness v1.0 Operator-Bundled Bootstrap Trust Root
description: Normative schema for the operator-bundled bootstrap channel defined in Core §5.3. The file is delivered out-of-band to the Runner via configuration management, signed-container base image, or equivalent trusted channel. The Runner MUST load and validate this file against the schema before loading any Agent Card. File MUST be named initial-trust.json and MUST be UTF-8 without BOM. Validation failure fails Runner startup with HostHardeningInsufficient (reason bootstrap-missing).

soaHarnessVersion.description: Binds this bootstrap file to a specific Core version. Runners MUST reject a bootstrap whose version is not in their §19.4 two-minor compatibility window.

publisher_kid.description: Release-signing key identifier (matches the kid in MANIFEST.json.jws header and the publisher_kid carried on at least one entry of security.trustAnchors in the Agent Card).

spki_sha256.description: Hex-lowercase SHA-256 of the release-signing key's SubjectPublicKeyInfo (RFC 5280).

issuer.description: RFC 4514 distinguished name of the certificate issuer, e.g., 'CN=SOA-WG Release CA, O=SOA-WG, C=US'.

issued_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of bootstrap file issuance. Recommended for rotation auditing per §5.3.1.

not_after.description: Optional hard expiry. If present, Runners MUST refuse to load an Agent Card after this instant and emit HostHardeningInsufficient (reason bootstrap-expired).

successor_publisher_kid.description: Optional; set during the §5.3.1 30-day rotation overlap window to announce the incoming publisher_kid. Runners MAY pre-trust the successor once observed but MUST continue accepting the current publisher_kid until not_after.

channel.description: Records which bootstrap channel of §5.3 produced this file. For operator-bundled files the value is 'operator-bundled'; SDK-pinned and DNSSEC-TXT producers MAY emit a file of this schema for diagnostic purposes but the Runner's authoritative channel is fixed at deployment time per §5.3.

signature.description: Optional detached signature over the JCS-RFC-8785-canonical bytes of this file (excluding the signature object itself). When present it MUST be produced by a key trusted by the deployment's configuration-management chain, not by publisher_kid — self-signing would be circular.
